repo_prior_art.cite: No catalogue entry gives a finite-population exposure-HT Bernstein interval with a fractional-chromatic dependency penalty; closest banked exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt is an asymptotic ego-cluster CLT/variance-envelope proposal.

published_prior_art.cite: AronowSamii2017 supplies exposure-HT randomization inference and asymptotics; PelekisRamonWang2017 supplies the fractional-color Hölder inequality; their composition yields the stated route but neither paper states this causal finite-sample interval.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal characterizes uniformly valid confidence-length lower bounds for exposure-overlap experiments; exp_ego_cluster_exposure_clt addresses an asymptotic CLT instead.

published_prior_art.cite: SandovalEtAl2026 gives ordinary-randomized-experiment nonasymptotic optimality, while the closest interference finite-sample comparators are TchetgenTchetgenVanderWeele2012 and RigdonHudgens2015; none gives the clique-block exposure-overlap lower-bound frontier.

novelty_flags.label: RigdonHudgens2015 / related_work
novelty_flags.one_line: The bibliography conflates Rigdon and Hudgens's Stat Med 34:924-935 paper, “Randomization inference for treatment effects on a binary outcome,” with their interference paper “Exact confidence intervals in the presence of interference,” Statistics & Probability Letters 105:130-135; repair the bibkey and comparisons.

novelty_flags.label: oeq:clique-block-minimax
novelty_flags.one_line: The lower-bound gap effectively has only SandovalEtAl2026 as a direct comparator; add and distinguish TchetgenTchetgenVanderWeele2012's finite-sample Hoeffding intervals under partial interference and the corrected RigdonHudgens2015 exact-interference paper.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:clique-block-minimax
soundness_flags.one_line: kappa_n and Vbar_n are model-specific but occur outside the supremum over m, while “nondegenerate range” and “up to the unavoidable linear Bernstein term” are undefined; fix a block design/exposure subclass or put the normalized expression inside the supremum and state the exact propensity range and residual term.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: State an explicit hard clique-block submodel: common within-block assignment coordinate, exposure maps, potential-outcome family, fixed propensity interval, and a Le Cam/two-point route; then formulate the lower bound with all model-dependent quantities correctly quantified.
Correct the Rigdon-Hudgens record and add Tchetgen-Tchetgen--VanderWeele (2012), separating exact partial-interference intervals from the proposed general-overlap minimax claim.
Sharpen the lower-bound target by specifying whether it matches only the square-root term or the full Bernstein radius, and give a regime where the linear term is dominated.

recommended_next_step: Revise the minimax OEQ into a fully quantified clique-block two-point target and repair the interference-interval literature record, then re-review at the field tier.
